title: Cloudflare Infrastructure & Internet Security
subtitle: How Cloudflare's edge network actually works — DNS, CDN, Workers, security, and the architecture under it
description: A practical course on Cloudflare built from their developer docs and engineering blog. Covers what the edge network does (DNS, CDN, DDoS, WAF, Zero Trust), how Workers and Pages run code at the edge, what 'serverless' actually means in Cloudflare's model, and the deployment patterns for using Cloudflare in production — without the marketing fluff.
audience: Developers, ops engineers, and technical founders deploying real workloads on Cloudflare. Some understanding of HTTP, DNS, and basic web architecture helps; we cover the rest.
prerequisites: Comfortable with the basics of HTTP, DNS, and how websites work
Familiarity with at least one programming language
A Cloudflare account (free tier is fine for following along)
outcomes: Explain what Cloudflare's edge network is and what it actually does
Configure DNS, SSL, and basic security on a real domain
Write and deploy Cloudflare Workers for edge logic
Set up Cloudflare Pages for a static or Jamstack site
Apply DDoS protection, WAF, and rate limiting
Use Zero Trust patterns for internal services
Pick the right Cloudflare product for the job and avoid common mistakes

modules.title: What Cloudflare Actually Is
modules.summary: The mental model: a global edge network sitting between users and your origin servers, plus a growing platform for running code at the edge.

lessons.title: Cloudflare in one diagram — what's actually happening
lessons.objective: Draw the request path from user to Cloudflare to origin and explain each hop.

lessons.title: Anycast and the edge — why Cloudflare is fast
lessons.objective: Understand the anycast routing model and the global PoP architecture.

lessons.title: What problems Cloudflare actually solves
lessons.objective: Distinguish marketing claims from the real benefits in your architecture.

lessons.title: What Cloudflare doesn't do (and what to use instead)
lessons.objective: Recognize the limits of the platform — when you need other tools.

modules.title: DNS, SSL, and Securing a Domain
modules.summary: The foundation — making your domain reachable, fast, and encrypted, with Cloudflare in front.

lessons.title: Cloudflare DNS — fast, free, and how it works
lessons.objective: Set up Cloudflare DNS for a domain and understand the record types.

lessons.title: Proxy mode vs. DNS-only — when traffic actually hits Cloudflare
lessons.objective: Decide which records to proxy and which to leave gray-cloud.

lessons.title: SSL/TLS — Universal SSL, modes, and origin certificates
lessons.objective: Configure SSL correctly end-to-end.

lessons.title: Common DNS and SSL mistakes — and how to avoid them
lessons.objective: Recognize and fix the patterns that break in production.

modules.title: Workers, Pages, and Edge Compute
modules.summary: How Cloudflare's compute platform works — Workers, Pages, durable objects, and the runtime model.

lessons.title: Workers — what they are and what they're for
lessons.objective: Write and deploy a basic Worker.

lessons.title: The Workers runtime — isolates, limits, and what's available

lessons.title: Cloudflare Pages — sites and the Jamstack model

lessons.title: Durable Objects, KV, R2, D1 — Cloudflare's storage primitives

modules.title: Security at the Edge

modules.title: Zero Trust and Production Patterns

lessons.title: Zero Trust — what it means and what Cloudflare provides
lessons.objective: Apply Zero Trust principles to internal apps.

lessons.title: Cloudflare Tunnel — internal services without public IPs
